user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Scheduled Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected initiated for Blue Yonder Airlines.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 08:54 EST
Subject: Re: Re: pipeline hygiene Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with pipeline hygiene. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-01 11:56 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-02-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft), Kevin (Blue Yonder Airlines)

[00:01:15] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is pipeline hygiene.

[00:05:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:07:00] Kevin (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:20:00] Kevin (Blue Yonder Airlines): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:23:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:27:00] Kevin (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:30:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:32:00] Kevin (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence
